meta.title: Golden Taan | Authentic Palmyra Palm Sugar - Sustainably Sourced in Thailand
meta.description: Discover Golden Taan, the premium, low-GI Palmyra Palm Sugar. 100% pure, unrefined, and sustainably sourced from our family farms in Ratchaburi, Thailand. The perfect healthy sugar alternative.

hero.title: Discover the True Taste of Nature
hero.subtitle: 100% Authentic Toddy Palm Sugar from the Heart of Thailand
hero.intro: Experience Golden Taan, where tradition meets purity. We bring you handcrafted palm sugar, harvested from wild Palmyra Palms (Borassus flabellifer) over 30 years old. Each batch is meticulously slow-boiled over a gentle wood fire, allowing it to be naturally caramelized and touched by smoke, creating a flavor profile that is truly unforgettable.

featuredProducts.title: Our Signature Products
featuredProducts.subtitle: Handcrafted from nature's purest nectar. Explore our best-sellers.

featuredRecipes.title: Taste the Inspiration
featuredRecipes.subtitle: Discover delicious ways to use Golden Taan in your kitchen, from sweet to savory.

1.title: Single-Origin Purity
1.text: Harvested exclusively from the heritage palm groves of Phetchaburi and Ratchaburi, our sugar offers a unique terroir you can taste.

2.title: Artisanal Process
2.text: Slow-boiled and never refined. We use zero preservatives or additives, ensuring you receive the pure, traditional smoky-caramel flavor.

3.title: Culinary Versatility
3.text: The perfect ingredient to elevate any dish. Stir it into coffee, bake it into cookies, or create a gourmet glaze for your favorite BBQ.

4.title: Eco-Conscious Packaging
4.text: Our products are protected in a natural kraft box with a vacuum-sealed inner pouch to guarantee absolute freshness on arrival.

partnership.title: Shipping & Wholesale Inquiries
partnership.content: We are proud to be a global partner for businesses seeking premium, authentic ingredients. We offer customizable bulk packaging and OEM services for international buyers, restaurants, and distributors. Contact us today to discover the Golden Taan difference.
partnership.button: Request an Export Quote or Sample Box

journeyInfographic.title: Our Journey of Purity
journeyInfographic.subtitle: From a heritage palm grove in the heart of Thailand to your kitchen, discover the story behind every golden drop.

sections.title: Our Origins
sections.content: Rooted in the rich soil of Ratchaburi, our story began over 30 years ago, built on a multi-generational passion for authentic palm sugar.

sections.title: Sustainable Farming
sections.content: We champion fair trade practices, partnering with over 40 local farming families to preserve traditional, eco-friendly cultivation methods.

sections.title: Artisanal Craftsmanship
sections.content: Every batch is slow-boiled for 8 hours in traditional kettles. This patient process develops its deep, smoky-caramel flavor with zero additives.

sections.title: From Thailand to the World
sections.content: We carefully pack and export our liquid gold, bringing the authentic taste of Thai tradition to health-conscious foodies in over 12 countries.

ourStory.authorBio: *Written by the Golden Taan Agricultural Team, combining decades of farming experience with a passion for sustainable practices.*

legacy.title: The Golden Taan Legacy: A Story of Authentic Palm Sugar
legacy.subtitle: From Our Village in Ratchaburi, Thailand to the World
legacy.rootsText: Nestled in the fertile plains of Ratchaburi, Golden Taan is born from a tradition passed down through generations. Our family-owned farms cultivate Palmyra Palms with deep respect for the land, using sustainable sugar farming practices that ensure the health of our ecosystem and the purity of our product.
legacy.harvestText: Our skilled farmers climb the towering palms to gently collect the sweet nectar, or 'Taan,' from the blossoms. This delicate process, performed by hand, ensures that only the finest quality sap is harvested. It is then carefully simmered in traditional kettles, a slow method that concentrates the flavour and preserves the vital nutrients.
legacy.promiseText: We guarantee a single-origin, unrefined product free from additives and preservatives. Every block, crystal, and drop of Golden Taan is a testament to our dedication to quality, authenticity, and the vibrant community we support. This is true, natural palm sugar.
legacy.link1: Learn about the Palmyra Palm Tree

palmyraPalm.title: The Palmyra Palm: A Natural Wonder
palmyraPalm.subtitle: Botany and History of *Borassus flabellifer*, the source of true Toddy Palm Sugar

1.title: Botanical Profile
1.content: The Palmyra Palm (Borassus flabellifer) is a majestic, fan-leafed palm that stands as a pillar of Southeast Asian ecosystems. Capable of growing up to 30 meters tall and living for over 100 years, it is a keystone species, providing food and habitat for a diverse range of wildlife. Its robust trunk and large, distinctive leaves make it an easily recognizable feature of the rural landscape. Unlike annual crops, the Palmyra Palm is a perennial that contributes to its environment for its entire long lifespan.

2.title: The Terroir of Ratchaburi
2.content: Just as the environment shapes the taste of fine wine, the terroir of Ratchaburi, Thailand, imparts a unique character to Golden Taan. The region's mineral-rich alluvial soil, fed by the Mae Klong River, provides our palms with essential nutrients that are then transferred to the nectar. This specific soil composition, combined with a tropical savanna climate, results in a palm sap with a uniquely complex flavour profile, distinguished by deep caramel notes and a subtle, earthy undertone that cannot be found elsewhere.

3.title: Historical Significance: The "Tree of Life"
3.content: For centuries, the Palmyra Palm has been revered in Thai culture as a 'tree of life,' a testament to its profound utility. Historically, every part of the tree was used. The sweet sap provided nourishment; a durable wood was used for construction and tools; the fibrous leaves were woven into baskets, mats, and even manuscripts; and various parts were utilized in traditional medicine. By choosing Golden Taan, you are not just enjoying a sweetener, but partaking in a rich cultural heritage that celebrates resourcefulness and a deep connection to the natural world.

harvestArt.title: From Blossom to Block: The Art of Harvesting Palmyra Palm Sugar
harvestArt.subtitle: The Science of a Time-Honored Tradition

1.title: The Harvest (Tapping)
1.content: The collection of Palmyra nectar is a remarkable display of generational skill. Our farmers, with years of experience, free-climb the towering palms, often with minimal equipment. At the top, they make a precise, paper-thin incision in the flower spathe—the protective sheath covering the blossoms. This act, known as tapping, requires immense skill; a cut too deep can harm the tree, while one too shallow yields no sap. This sustainable, manual method ensures the tree remains healthy and productive for decades.

2.title: The Science of Simmering
2.content: From a food science perspective, our traditional kettle-simmering is a masterclass in flavour development. The slow, controlled heating of the fresh nectar accomplishes two crucial chemical reactions. Firstly, caramelization occurs, breaking down the sugars and forming hundreds of new aromatic compounds that create the rich, complex taste. Secondly, the Maillard reaction—a reaction between amino acids and reducing sugars—generates deep, savory, and roasted notes. This slow process, impossible to replicate with industrial evaporators, preserves volatile nutrients and builds a flavour profile of unparalleled depth.

3.title: From Nectar to Crystal
3.content: The transformation of the watery, translucent sap into its final form is a carefully controlled process. As the nectar simmers, water evaporates and it thickens into a rich, dark syrup. The critical moment comes when the syrup reaches the perfect supersaturated state. The heat is cut, and the syrup is agitated. This encourages nucleation, where sugar molecules begin to arrange themselves into microscopic seed crystals. With precise timing and temperature control, these crystals grow, forming the final texture of our sugar—either the fine crystals of our powder or the dense structure of our traditional blocks.

commitment.title: Our Commitment: Sustainable Sugar for Planet & People
commitment.subtitle: Sustainability and Community at Our Core

1.title: Ecological Sustainability
1.content: Palmyra Palm cultivation is an inherently sustainable agricultural model. As a perennial crop, the trees are not replanted annually, which means the soil is left undisturbed, preventing erosion and preserving its delicate microbial ecosystem. Their deep, powerful root systems improve soil structure and water retention. Furthermore, these palms are naturally drought-resistant and thrive without chemical pesticides or fertilizers, placing a minimal burden on the environment. This stands in stark contrast to the high ecological impact of many annual sugar crops, which often require intensive tilling, irrigation, and chemical inputs.

2.title: Community Empowerment
2.content: Golden Taan is built on a foundation of fair trade principles. We partner directly with local farming families, ensuring they receive stable, premium wages that reflect the skilled, artisanal nature of their work. This model bypasses intermediaries, allowing the local economy to benefit directly from the export of its unique heritage product. By investing in our community, we help preserve generational skills that might otherwise be lost, fostering economic stability and cultural continuity for the people of Ratchaburi.

3.title: Purity & Quality Assurance
3.content: Our 'single-origin' promise is your guarantee of unparalleled quality and traceability. Every batch of Golden Taan can be traced back to our community farms in Ratchaburi. This ensures a consistent, authentic flavour profile that is a true expression of its unique terroir. We are unconditionally committed to a 100% natural product. This means no chemical pesticides, no bleaching agents, no preservatives, and no anti-caking additives. It's a clean-label promise that meets the highest global standards for pure, wholesome food.

communityImpact.title: Our Community Impact
communityImpact.subtitle: Beyond producing exceptional palm sugar, Golden Taan is deeply invested in the prosperity of our community. We believe that true success is shared, creating a sustainable cycle of empowerment, preservation, and economic well-being.

items.title: Empowering Local Families
items.text: We partner with over 40 local farming families, providing stable, fair-trade wages that are 15% higher than the market rate. This ensures economic security and allows families to thrive.

items.title: Preserving Generational Skills
items.text: The art of palm tapping is a heritage skill. By making it a viable and respected profession, we help preserve this invaluable cultural knowledge for future generations.

items.title: Boosting the Local Economy
items.text: Our operations contribute directly to the Ratchaburi economy, generating sustainable income and supporting over 2,000 rural households, fostering a vibrant and self-sufficient community.

items.title: A Partnership for the Future
items.text: Each purchase of Golden Taan is a direct investment in this ecosystem of mutual respect and shared prosperity, ensuring the legacy of our community continues to grow.

products.title: Our Authentic Palmyra Palm Sugar Products
products.subtitle: Explore our range of 100% pure, unrefined Toddy Palm Sugar, crafted with care from our palms to your pantry.

1.title: Golden Taan – Traditional Palmyra Palm Sugar Block
1.alt: A solid block of traditional Golden Taan Palmyra Palm Sugar wrapped in a palm leaf.
1.description: Authentic solid palm sugar, handcrafted into traditional blocks. Available wrapped in fragrant palm leaves or in convenient ziplock bags.
1.targetUsers: Traditional Thai dessert shops, households, local restaurants.
1.usp: Rich, intense aroma. Ideal for authentic Thai desserts like khanom tan and mor gaeng.
1.market: Local markets and export destinations focused on traditional flavor profiles (e.g., Japan, Vietnam).

2.title: Golden Taan – Natural Palm Sugar Powder
2.alt: Fine, granulated Golden Taan Palmyra Palm Sugar Powder in a clear bowl.
2.description: Our versatile palm sugar powder, finely ground for easy mixing. A perfect 1:1 replacement for white or brown sugar.
2.targetUsers: Modern restaurants, chefs, cafés, global consumers.
2.usp: A gentle, aromatic healthy sugar alternative to white sugar in both savory and sweet dishes.
2.market: Global kitchen-friendly markets such as the U.S., Europe, and South Korea.

3.title: Golden Taan – Toddy Palm Sugar Syrup
3.alt: Thick, amber-colored Golden Taan Toddy Palm Sugar Syrup in a glass bottle.
3.description: A rich, pourable palm sugar syrup, bottled for convenience. Perfect for drizzling over desserts, pancakes, or stirring into beverages.
3.targetUsers: Coffee shops, restaurants, health-conscious consumers, bakers.
3.usp: Perfect as a topping for coffee, tea, shaved ice, or fusion desserts.
3.market: Specialty markets such as baristas, home cafés, and the clean-eating food sector.

4.title: Golden Taan – Certified Organic Toddy Palm Sugar
4.alt: Premium packaging of Golden Taan organic Toddy Palm Sugar with USDA and EU Organic logos.
4.description: Our finest Toddy Palm Sugar, certified organic by USDA and EU standards, presented in premium export-ready packaging.
4.targetUsers: Health-conscious consumers, vegan restaurants, premium export markets.
4.usp: 100% chemical-free, sustainable sugar farming, traceable production.
4.market: North America, Europe, South Korea, and high-end clientele.

5.title: Golden Taan – Palm Sugar Fusion Flavors
5.alt: A collection of Golden Taan fusion flavored palm sugars, including cinnamon and roasted coconut.
5.description: An innovative twist on tradition. Our palm sugar infused with natural flavors like roasted coconut or warming cinnamon for a unique taste experience.
5.targetUsers: Bakeries, Modern Thai fusion restaurants, homemade dessert shops.
5.usp: Unique and differentiated. Introduces new flavor notes to elevate signature dishes.
5.market: Creative food industries, dessert innovation markets, and experimental restaurants.

benefits.title: The Wholesome Difference of a Low GI Sweetener
benefits.subtitle: Why Choose Golden Taan as a Healthy Sugar Alternative?

1.title: A Low Glycemic Index (GI) Sweetener
1.content: Unlike refined sugars that cause sharp spikes in blood glucose levels, Golden Taan has a naturally low glycemic index (approx. 35). This means it is absorbed more slowly into the bloodstream, providing a steadier, more sustained release of energy without the subsequent 'crash.' This characteristic makes it a smarter choice for individuals managing blood sugar levels, athletes seeking sustained energy, and anyone looking for a more balanced sweetener. (Source: Food Science and Nutrition Journals).

2.title: A Rich Source of Essential Minerals
2.content: Our minimal, traditional processing preserves the natural goodness of the palm sap. Golden Taan is a notable source of essential minerals often stripped away from highly processed sweeteners. It contains significant levels of Potassium, which is vital for nerve function and blood pressure regulation; Iron, crucial for oxygen transport; and Zinc, important for immune function.

3.title: Natural Electrolytes for Hydration
3.content: The same minerals that make Golden Taan nutritious also make it a source of natural electrolytes. This makes it an excellent ingredient for creating natural hydration beverages or for use in post-workout recovery snacks, helping to replenish what the body loses during exercise.

4.title: The Clean-Label Promise: Unrefined & Pure
4.content: Experience the difference of a truly natural sugar. Golden Taan is 100% pure, unrefined, and free from bone char, anti-caking agents, and chemical additives. It is processed without the use of any chemicals, making it a clean, vegan-friendly choice for the conscious consumer.

recipes.title: Palmyra Palm Sugar Recipes & Usage Ideas
recipes.subtitle: From traditional desserts to modern cocktails, discover how to use our natural palm sugar.

1.name: For the Barista: Golden Taan Caramel Latte
1.alt: A warm latte in a glass mug, drizzled with Golden Taan palm sugar caramel sauce.
1.description: Elevate your morning coffee. The deep caramel notes of our palm sugar dissolve perfectly to create a rich, complex latte that rivals any specialty café.
recipeIngredient: 1 tbsp Golden Taan Palm Sugar Syrup

recipeInstructions.name: Step 1: Combine
recipeInstructions.text: Pour the Golden Taan Palm Sugar Syrup into your favorite mug. Brew one shot of espresso directly over the syrup and stir to combine.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 2: Froth Milk
recipeInstructions.text: Froth or steam your milk until it reaches the desired texture and temperature.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 3: Assemble & Garnish
recipeInstructions.text: Gently pour the steamed milk into the mug. Garnish with a pinch of cinnamon if desired and enjoy your delicious palm sugar latte.

2.name: For the Baker: Gluten-Free Palmyra Blondies
2.alt: A batch of freshly baked gluten-free blondies made with palmyra palm sugar.
2.description: Add a moist texture and a sophisticated, butterscotch-like sweetness to your baking. Our palm sugar is a perfect 1:1 replacement for brown sugar.
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Golden Taan Palm Sugar Powder

recipeInstructions.name: Step 1: Mix Wet Ingredients
recipeInstructions.text: In a large bowl, whisk together the Golden Taan Palm Sugar Powder and melted coconut oil. Add the egg and vanilla extract, and mix until smooth.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 2: Add Dry Ingredients
recipeInstructions.text: In a separate bowl, combine the gluten-free flour, baking powder, and salt. Gradually add the dry ingredients to the wet ingredients and mix until just combined.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 3: Bake
recipeInstructions.text: Pour the batter into a greased 8x8 inch baking pan. Bake at 175°C (350°F) for 20-25 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean. Let cool before cutting.

3.name: For the Chef: Savory Palm Sugar Glaze for Salmon
3.alt: A pan-seared salmon fillet with a shiny, caramelized glaze made from palm sugar.
3.description: Balance savory and sweet. Create a simple, elegant glaze with Golden Taan syrup, soy sauce, and ginger to give your fish or chicken a perfectly caramelized crust.
recipeIngredient: 2 tbsp Golden Taan Palm Sugar Syrup

recipeInstructions.name: Step 1: Make the Glaze
recipeInstructions.text: In a small bowl, whisk together the Golden Taan syrup, soy sauce, ginger, and garlic.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 2: Sear the Salmon
recipeInstructions.text: Season the salmon fillets with salt and pepper. Heat a skillet over medium-high heat with a little oil. Sear the salmon, skin-side down, for 4-5 minutes until crispy.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 3: Glaze and Finish
recipeInstructions.text: Flip the salmon, then pour the glaze over the fillets. Cook for another 2-3 minutes, spooning the glaze over the fish, until the salmon is cooked through and the glaze has thickened.

4.name: For the Mixologist: Smoky Old Fashioned
4.alt: A classic Old Fashioned cocktail in a rocks glass, garnished with an orange peel.
4.description: Reinvent a classic cocktail. Use our palm sugar syrup to add a smoky depth and complexity that complements fine whiskey.
recipeIngredient: 10ml (1/3 oz) Golden Taan Palm Sugar Syrup

recipeInstructions.name: Step 1: Combine Ingredients
recipeInstructions.text: In a mixing glass filled with ice, combine the whiskey, Golden Taan syrup, and bitters.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 2: Stir
recipeInstructions.text: Stir the mixture with a bar spoon for about 20-30 seconds until well-chilled.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 3: Strain and Garnish
recipeInstructions.text: Strain the cocktail into a rocks glass over a large ice cube. Express the oils from an orange peel over the drink, then drop it in as a garnish.

5.name: Khanom Kluay (Banana Cake with Golden TAAN)
5.alt: Soft steamed banana cakes in a traditional wrapper.
5.description: For banana lovers: A classic soft and sweet banana cake, with an added fragrant depth from Golden Taan Palm Sugar. A healthier alternative to white sugar.
recipeIngredient: Banana leaves for lining cups (or paper molds)

recipeInstructions.name: Step 1: Mix
recipeInstructions.text: Mash the bananas thoroughly. Mix with Palm Sugar and stir until dissolved.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 2: Combine Flours
recipeInstructions.text: Add both types of flour and the baking powder. Mix well.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 3: Finish Batter
recipeInstructions.text: Pour in the coconut cream and stir until the batter is smooth.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 4: Steam
recipeInstructions.text: Pour into molds and steam for 25 minutes, or until cooked and risen.

6.name: Thapthim Krop (Thai Ruby Dessert with Palm Sugar)
6.alt: Red water chestnut 'rubies' in sweet coconut milk.
6.description: A refreshing dessert for a hot day: Crispy, vividly colored water chestnuts served in a fragrant sweet coconut milk simmered with Palm Sugar for a smoother, richer flavor.
recipeIngredient: Natural red food coloring (e.g., beetroot juice)

recipeInstructions.name: Step 1: Color & Coat
recipeInstructions.text: Soak the water chestnuts in red coloring until the desired color is achieved, then coat them thoroughly in tapioca starch.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 2: Cook Rubies
recipeInstructions.text: Boil the coated chestnuts in water until they float to the surface. Scoop them out and plunge into ice-cold water.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 3: Make Syrup
recipeInstructions.text: Simmer the Palm Sugar with coconut cream and salt until dissolved. Let it cool.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 4: Serve
recipeInstructions.text: Serve the 'rubies' in the sweet coconut milk with crushed ice.

7.name: Khanom Piak Poon (Steamed Rice Flour Custard)
7.alt: Soft, pandan-scented rice flour custard squares.
7.description: Aromatic with pandan: A smooth, soft custard, vibrant green from fresh pandan leaves, with a deep, mellow sweetness from Palm Sugar.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 1: Mix Flour
recipeInstructions.text: Dissolve the flours in pandan juice and strain for a smooth texture.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 2: Heat Syrup
recipeInstructions.text: Mix coconut cream with Palm Sugar over low heat until dissolved.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 3: Combine & Steam
recipeInstructions.text: Combine all ingredients, pour into a steaming mold, and steam for 15-20 minutes.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 4: Serve
recipeInstructions.text: Let cool before serving, topped with grated coconut.

8.name: Sangkaya (Coconut Custard with Palm Sugar)
8.alt: Smooth and rich coconut custard, often served with sticky rice.
8.description: Rich and velvety: A classic Sangkaya custard made with Palm Sugar instead of coconut sugar, adding a unique fragrance and a melt-in-your-mouth texture.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 1: Whisk
recipeInstructions.text: Whisk the eggs and Palm Sugar together until well combined.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 2: Add Cream
recipeInstructions.text: Add coconut cream, salt, and knotted pandan leaves. Stir until smooth.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 3: Strain & Pour
recipeInstructions.text: Strain the mixture and pour into small cups or foil containers.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 4: Steam
recipeInstructions.text: Steam for 25-30 minutes, or until the custard is set and smooth.

9.name: Khao Niao Moon (Sweet Sticky Rice with Golden TAAN)
9.alt: Sweet sticky rice served with fresh mango.
9.description: A perfectly fragrant recipe: Using Palm Sugar enhances the delicate aroma of the coconut milk. Pairs beautifully with fresh mango or durian.
recipeIngredient: 2 cups 'khao niao kiew ngu' sticky rice (soaked for 6 hours)

recipeInstructions.name: Step 1: Steam Rice
recipeInstructions.text: Steam the soaked sticky rice until cooked through.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 2: Make Coconut Milk
recipeInstructions.text: Simmer the coconut cream, Palm Sugar, and salt until everything is dissolved.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 3: Mix
recipeInstructions.text: Pour the hot coconut milk mixture over the cooked sticky rice. Stir to combine and let it sit for 30 minutes to absorb.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 4: Serve
recipeInstructions.text: Serve with fresh fruit or toasted mung beans.

10.name: Met Khanun (Sweet Mung Bean Yolk Drops)
10.alt: Golden yolk-coated mung bean paste sweets shaped like jackfruit seeds.
10.description: A delicate, sweet, and rich treat: Mashed mung bean paste mixed with egg yolk and palm sugar, creating a well-rounded, fragrant sweetness.
recipeIngredient: 1 cup steamed and mashed split mung beans

recipeInstructions.name: Step 1: Make Paste
recipeInstructions.text: Mix the mashed mung beans, flour, and egg yolks until smooth.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 2: Shape
recipeInstructions.text: Shape the paste into small ovals resembling jackfruit seeds. Set aside.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 3: Make Syrup
recipeInstructions.text: Boil water and Palm Sugar until dissolved, then reduce the heat.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 4: Cook
recipeInstructions.text: Gently drop the 'seeds' into the syrup and cook until they float and are cooked through. Let cool and serve in the syrup.

11.name: Khao Tom Mat (Banana Sticky Rice Wrap)
11.alt: Steamed sticky rice and banana wrapped in banana leaves.
11.description: Fragrant banana wrapped in leaves: Sticky rice sweetened with a coconut milk and Palm Sugar mixture for a gentler aroma, all enveloped in the fragrant steam of banana leaves.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 1: Steam Rice
recipeInstructions.text: Steam the soaked sticky rice until cooked.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 2: Make Coconut Milk
recipeInstructions.text: Simmer the coconut cream, Palm Sugar, and salt until dissolved.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 3: Mix
recipeInstructions.text: Mix the sweet coconut milk into the cooked sticky rice.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 4: Wrap & Steam
recipeInstructions.text: Spoon the rice onto a banana leaf, place a banana piece in the center, and wrap into a parcel. Steam for 30 minutes.

12.name: For Meat Lovers: Grilled Pork Chops with Apple Palm Sugar Sauce
12.alt: Grilled pork chops with a shiny apple and palm sugar glaze.
12.description: Elevate ordinary pork chops to a restaurant-quality dish with a glaze that combines the sweet aroma of palm sugar with the fresh tang of apples, making the pork tender, juicy, and infused with a caramel scent.
recipeIngredient: 3 tbsp Golden Taan Palm Sugar Syrup

recipeInstructions.name: Step 1: Make the Sauce
recipeInstructions.text: In a small saucepan, combine the palm sugar syrup, apple cider, and mustard. Simmer over low heat until the sauce thickens slightly, then stir in the rosemary.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 2: Grill the Pork Chops
recipeInstructions.text: Season the pork chops with salt and pepper. Grill on a hot pan or grill until almost cooked through (about 3-4 minutes per side).

recipeInstructions.name: Step 3: Glaze and Rest
recipeInstructions.text: Brush the sauce generously over the pork chops during the final moments of grilling. Cook for another minute per side until the glaze is beautifully caramelized. Let them rest for 5 minutes before serving.

13.name: For Bakers: Salted Caramel Palm Sugar Tart
13.alt: A decadent salted caramel tart made with palm sugar, sliced and ready to serve.
13.description: Create a next-level caramel tart with the deep, complex flavor of authentic palm sugar. It offers a rich sweetness perfectly balanced by sea salt, making it an impressive dessert for any occasion.
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Golden Taan Palm Sugar Powder

recipeInstructions.name: Step 1: Bake Tart
recipeInstructions.text: Bake the tart shell until golden brown. Let it cool completely.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 2: Make Caramel
recipeInstructions.text: In a medium saucepan, melt the palm sugar powder over medium heat until it turns a beautiful amber color. Carefully pour in the heavy cream (be cautious of bubbling) and whisk until smooth. Remove from heat, then add the butter and sea salt, stirring until the butter is fully melted.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 3: Assemble
recipeInstructions.text: Pour the warm caramel into the prepared tart shell, spreading it evenly. Refrigerate for at least 2 hours to allow the caramel to set.

14.name: For the Health-Conscious: Quinoa Salad with Balsamic Palm Sugar Dressing
14.alt: A vibrant quinoa salad with fresh vegetables.
14.description: A dressing that transforms a simple salad. The mellow sweetness of palm sugar balances the sharpness of balsamic vinegar, creating a harmonious flavor that pairs perfectly with quinoa and fresh vegetables.
recipeIngredient: 1 tbsp Golden Taan Palm Sugar Syrup

recipeInstructions.name: Step 1: Make the Dressing
recipeInstructions.text: In a jar or small bowl, combine the palm sugar syrup and balsamic vinegar. Whisk until blended. Slowly drizzle in the olive oil while whisking continuously to emulsify. Season with salt and pepper.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 2: Assemble Salad
recipeInstructions.text: In a large salad bowl, combine the salad greens and cooked quinoa.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 3: Toss and Serve
recipeInstructions.text: Pour the dressing over the salad and toss gently to combine everything before serving.

15.name: For the Bartender: 'Taan Old Fashioned' Cocktail
15.alt: A sophisticated Taan Old Fashioned cocktail in a rocks glass.
15.description: A classic cocktail, reinterpreted. The palm sugar syrup provides a dimensional sweetness with a faint smoky note that pairs wonderfully with fine whiskey, creating a memorable drinking experience.
recipeIngredient: 1/4 oz Golden Taan Palm Sugar Syrup

recipeInstructions.name: Step 1: Combine
recipeInstructions.text: In a mixing glass, add ice, palm sugar syrup, and bitters.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 2: Stir
recipeInstructions.text: Pour in the whiskey. Use a bar spoon to stir for about 20-30 seconds until the glass is well-chilled.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 3: Serve
recipeInstructions.text: Strain the mixture into an Old Fashioned glass with a large ice cube. Express the oils from an orange peel over the drink and drop it in.

16.name: For the Chef: Seared Duck Breast with Berry Palm Sugar Sauce
16.alt: Perfectly seared duck breast with a rich berry sauce.
16.description: A luxurious dish that's easy to make. The sweet-tart berry sauce is balanced and deepened by the palm sugar, perfectly glazing the crispy-skinned duck.
recipeIngredient: 2 tbsp Golden Taan Palm Sugar Powder

recipeInstructions.name: Step 1: Sear the Duck
recipeInstructions.text: Score the duck skin, season with salt and pepper. Place skin-side down in a cold pan over low heat to render the fat until the skin is golden and crispy. Sear the other side until cooked to your liking. Remove and rest.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 2: Make the Sauce
recipeInstructions.text: In the same pan, pour off excess duck fat. Deglaze with red wine, scraping up any bits. Add the mixed berries, palm sugar, and thyme.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 3: Simmer
recipeInstructions.text: Simmer the sauce over medium heat until the berries break down and the sauce thickens. Taste and adjust seasoning. Pour over the sliced duck breast.

17.name: For BBQ Lovers: Smoked Palm Sugar BBQ Sauce
17.alt: A jar of homemade, dark, and rich BBQ sauce.
17.description: A homemade BBQ sauce with a unique flavor profile. The smoky sweetness of palm sugar provides an excellent base, creating a rich sauce that perfectly coats ribs or grilled chicken.
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Golden Taan Palm Sugar Powder

recipeInstructions.name: Step 1: Combine Ingredients
recipeInstructions.text: Place all ingredients in a saucepan.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 2: Simmer
recipeInstructions.text: Bring to a simmer over medium heat, stirring until the sugar dissolves. Reduce heat to low and cook for 20-30 minutes until the sauce has thickened and the flavors have melded.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 3: Use or Store
recipeInstructions.text: Let the sauce cool. It can be stored in the refrigerator for up to a week. Ideal for brushing on meat during grilling or as a dipping sauce.

18.name: For Bakers: Soft-Baked Chocolate Chip Palm Sugar Cookies
18.alt: A stack of soft and chewy chocolate chip cookies.
18.description: Transform ordinary chocolate chip cookies. Palm sugar provides moisture and a deep, toffee-like flavor, resulting in a cookie with a softer, chewier texture and a more aromatic taste than ever before.
recipeIngredient: 3/4 cup Golden Taan Palm Sugar Powder

recipeInstructions.name: Step 1: Mix Wet Ingredients
recipeInstructions.text: In a mixing bowl, whisk the melted butter and palm sugar together. Then, add the egg and vanilla extract, mixing until combined.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 2: Add Dry Ingredients
recipeInstructions.text: Sift in the flour, baking soda, and salt. Mix until just combined, then fold in the chocolate chips.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 3: Bake
recipeInstructions.text: Scoop rounded tablespoons of dough onto a baking sheet. Bake at 175°C (350°F) for 10-12 minutes, until the edges are golden brown.

19.name: For Brunch Lovers: Candied Bacon with Palm Sugar & Black Pepper
19.alt: Crispy strips of candied bacon on a wire rack.
19.description: A wildly popular snack or side dish that perfectly combines the saltiness of bacon, the sweet aroma of palm sugar, and a slight kick from black pepper.
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup Golden Taan Palm Sugar Powder

recipeInstructions.name: Step 1: Prepare Bacon
recipeInstructions.text: Arrange the bacon strips on a wire rack set over a baking sheet.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 2: Coat with Sugar
recipeInstructions.text: Mix the palm sugar with the black pepper (and cayenne). Sprinkle evenly over the bacon strips.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 3: Bake
recipeInstructions.text: Bake at 200°C (400°F) for 15-20 minutes, or until the bacon is crispy and the sugar is beautifully caramelized.

20.name: For Chefs: Roasted Carrots & Sweet Potatoes with Palm Sugar Glaze
20.alt: A bowl of roasted carrots and sweet potatoes with a shiny glaze.
20.description: A simple yet elegant side dish. The sweetness of the palm sugar brings out the natural flavors of the root vegetables and gives them an appetizing, shiny glaze.
recipeIngredient: 2 tbsp Golden Taan Palm Sugar Syrup

recipeInstructions.name: Step 1: Toss
recipeInstructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the carrots and sweet potatoes. Drizzle with olive oil and palm sugar syrup. Season with salt, pepper, and fresh thyme. Toss to coat.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 2: Roast
recipeInstructions.text: Spread the vegetables in a single layer on a baking sheet. Roast at 200°C (400°F) for 25-30 minutes, or until tender and caramelized, flipping once halfway through.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 3: Serve
recipeInstructions.text: Serve hot as a side dish.

21.name: For the Health-Conscious: Homemade Palm Sugar Granola
21.alt: A jar filled with homemade granola clusters.
21.description: A homemade granola that's more fragrant and healthier than store-bought versions. Palm sugar provides just the right amount of sweetness and helps create satisfyingly crunchy clusters.
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup Golden Taan Palm Sugar Syrup

recipeInstructions.name: Step 1: Mix Dry Ingredients
recipeInstructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the oats, nuts, seeds, and cinnamon.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 2: Mix Wet Ingredients
recipeInstructions.text: In a small bowl, warm the coconut oil and palm sugar syrup together until combined.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 3: Bake
recipeInstructions.text: Pour the wet mixture over the dry ingredients and toss to coat. Spread onto a baking sheet and bake at 150°C (300°F) for 30-40 minutes, stirring every 15 minutes, until golden and crisp. Let cool completely before storing in a jar.

22.name: For the Ultimate Dip: Nam Pla Wan (Sweet Fish Sauce Dip)
22.alt: Nam Pla Wan dipping sauce served with grilled catfish.
22.description: An essential dipping sauce for sour mangoes or grilled catfish with neem. The quality of Nam Pla Wan is measured by its concentration and the aroma of palm sugar, and Golden Taan provides a perfectly balanced sweet-salty taste and ideal thickness.
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Golden Taan Palm Sugar Block
Bird's eye chilies, sliced, to taste

recipeInstructions.name: Step 1: Simmer the Sauce
recipeInstructions.text: In a small pot, add palm sugar, fish sauce, and tamarind paste. Set over low heat and gently simmer until the sugar dissolves and the sauce thickens.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 2: Add Aromatics
recipeInstructions.text: Once the sauce has thickened, turn off the heat.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 3: Serve
recipeInstructions.text: Add the sliced shallots, ground dried shrimp, and sliced chilies. Stir to combine. Let it cool and serve with sour mangoes or grilled catfish.

23.name: For a Royal Classic: Mee Krob (Crispy Noodles)
23.alt: A mound of crispy Mee Krob noodles, garnished with shrimp and tofu.
23.description: An ancient, elaborate snack. The secret to the perfectly balanced sweet, sour, and salty sauce for these crispy noodles lies in using authentic palm sugar, which gives a mellow sweetness and a beautiful, natural color.
recipeIngredient: Fresh shrimp, tofu, and small pieces of pork
1/2 cup Golden Taan Palm Sugar Block
Zest of a som saa (citrus hystrix), if available

recipeInstructions.name: Step 1: Fry the Noodles
recipeInstructions.text: Heat oil until very hot. Fry the drained vermicelli in small batches until they puff up and turn golden and crispy. Remove and set aside.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 2: Make the Sauce
recipeInstructions.text: In a new pan, sauté shallots with a little oil until fragrant. Add the palm sugar block, fish sauce, and tamarind paste. Simmer on low heat until the sugar dissolves and the sauce thickens.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 3: Add Proteins and Toss
recipeInstructions.text: Add shrimp, tofu, and pork to the sauce and stir-fry until cooked. Turn off the heat. Quickly add the crispy noodles and toss to coat them evenly in the sauce. Garnish with som saa zest before serving.

24.name: For a Main Course: Pla Rad Prik (Fish with Three-Flavor Sauce)
24.alt: A whole fried fish covered in a glossy, red three-flavor chili sauce.
24.description: A fried fish dish renowned for its three-flavored sauce (sour, sweet, salty). The heart of the sauce is the dimensional sweetness of palm sugar, which glazes the fried fish, making it glossy and intensely flavorful.
recipeIngredient: 3 tbsp Golden Taan Palm Sugar Block
2 tbsp pounded red spur chilies and garlic

recipeInstructions.name: Step 1: Fry the Fish
recipeInstructions.text: Score the fish and deep-fry in hot oil until golden and crispy on both sides. Remove and set aside.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 2: Make the Three-Flavor Sauce
recipeInstructions.text: In a pan, sauté the pounded chili-garlic paste in a little oil until fragrant. Then add the palm sugar, fish sauce, and tamarind paste.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 3: Simmer and Glaze
recipeInstructions.text: Simmer the sauce on low heat until it thickens. Taste to ensure a balanced three-flavor profile. Pour the hot sauce over the fried fish before serving.

25.name: For a Special Meal: Gaeng Massaman (Massaman Curry)
25.alt: A rich and fragrant bowl of Massaman chicken curry with potatoes and peanuts.
25.description: A Thai curry with foreign influences and a unique flavor. Palm sugar is the secret ingredient that balances the curry, giving it a deep, mellow sweetness that harmonizes with the heat of the spices.
recipeIngredient: 1 cup coconut cream and 2 cups coconut milk
1-2 tbsp Golden Taan Palm Sugar Block

recipeInstructions.name: Step 1: Sauté the Curry Paste
recipeInstructions.text: In a pot, heat the coconut cream until the oil separates beautifully. Add the Massaman curry paste and sauté until fragrant.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 2: Add Chicken and Season
recipeInstructions.text: Add the chicken and stir-fry with the paste until the surface is cooked. Then, add the coconut milk. Season with fish sauce, tamarind paste, and palm sugar.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 3: Simmer
recipeInstructions.text: Add the onions and potatoes. Simmer on low heat until the chicken and potatoes are tender. Finally, add the roasted peanuts before turning off the heat.

26.name: For Southern Food Lovers: Moo Hong (Phuket-Style Braised Pork Belly)
26.alt: Tender, dark braised pork belly (Moo Hong) in a bowl.
26.description: A Phuket-style braised pork belly dish that's melt-in-your-mouth tender. Its harmonious salty-sweet flavor comes from authentic palm sugar simmered with soy sauce and spices until it infuses the meat.
recipeIngredient: 2 tbsp pounded 'three-buddy' paste (coriander root, garlic, pepper)
2-3 tbsp Golden Taan Palm Sugar Block

recipeInstructions.name: Step 1: Sauté the Paste
recipeInstructions.text: In a pot, sauté the 'three-buddy' paste with a little oil until fragrant.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 2: Add Pork and Season
recipeInstructions.text: Add the pork belly and stir-fry until the surface is seared. Season with dark soy sauce, light soy sauce, and palm sugar. Stir until the sugar dissolves and coats the pork pieces.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 3: Braise
recipeInstructions.text: Add water to cover the pork and add the cinnamon stick. Bring to a boil, then reduce to low heat. Braise for 1.5 - 2 hours, or until the pork is perfectly tender.

27.name: For Isaan Food Lovers: Nam Jim Jaew (Jaew Dipping Sauce)
27.alt: A small bowl of Nam Jim Jaew, a spicy Thai dipping sauce.
27.description: The perfect spicy dip for grilled pork neck or 'crying tiger' beef. The secret to a delicious Jaew is the balance of sour, salty, and sweet, for which palm sugar provides a softer sweetness than cane sugar.
recipeIngredient: 1 tbsp Golden Taan Palm Sugar Powder
Sliced shallots and chopped spring onions/coriander

recipeInstructions.name: Step 1: Make the Base
recipeInstructions.text: In a mixing bowl, add palm sugar powder, fish sauce, and tamarind paste. Stir until the sugar is completely dissolved.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 2: Add Aromatics
recipeInstructions.text: Add the toasted rice powder, chili flakes, shallots, and spring onions/coriander. Stir to combine.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 3: Serve
recipeInstructions.text: Let it sit for a moment to allow the toasted rice powder to swell slightly. Serve with various grilled meats.

28.name: For Noodle Dishes: Pad Thai Sauce
28.alt: A jar of rich, dark Pad Thai sauce.
28.description: The heart of a delicious Pad Thai is its meticulously prepared sauce. Authentic palm sugar is an indispensable ingredient for creating a Pad Thai sauce with a perfectly balanced sweet, salty, and sour flavor and a beautiful color.
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Golden Taan Palm Sugar Block

recipeInstructions.name: Step 1: Simmer the Sauce
recipeInstructions.text: Combine all ingredients in a pot over low heat.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 2: Stir until Combined
recipeInstructions.text: Stir the mixture continuously until the palm sugar has completely dissolved and the sauce begins to thicken.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 3: Store
recipeInstructions.text: Let the sauce cool completely. It can be stored in a jar in the refrigerator, ready to use for stir-frying Pad Thai noodles anytime.

29.name: For a Snack: Miang Kham (Leaf-Wrapped Bites)
29.alt: A platter of Miang Kham ingredients ready for wrapping.
29.description: A traditional Thai snack rich in herbs. The rich dipping sauce, with its sweet and salty notes, is the star of this dish, requiring authentic palm sugar to achieve the right texture and aroma.
recipeIngredient: Toasted coconut, roasted peanuts, ginger, shallots, lime (diced), bird's eye chilies
1 cup Golden Taan Palm Sugar Block

recipeInstructions.name: Step 1: Make the Dipping Sauce
recipeInstructions.text: Pound galangal, lemongrass, and shrimp paste until fine. Simmer with palm sugar and water over low heat until thick and sticky. Let cool.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 2: Prepare the Fillings
recipeInstructions.text: Prepare the various ingredients: toasted coconut, peanuts, ginger, shallots, lime, and chilies.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 3: Serve
recipeInstructions.text: Arrange the betel leaves with all the fillings and the cooled dipping sauce. To eat, wrap the fillings in a leaf and top with the sauce.

30.name: For a Side: Tod Mun Dipping Sauce (Fish Cake Dip)
30.alt: A sweet and sour cucumber relish for Thai fish cakes.
30.description: The 'ajat' dipping sauce for fish or shrimp cakes. Its refreshing sweet and sour flavor comes from palm sugar simmered with vinegar, balanced with cucumber and spur chilies.
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Golden Taan Palm Sugar Powder
Sliced cucumber, sliced shallots, sliced red spur chilies

recipeInstructions.name: Step 1: Make the Syrup
recipeInstructions.text: Combine palm sugar, vinegar, and salt in a pot. Heat on low, stirring until the sugar dissolves completely. Let it cool completely.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 2: Add Vegetables
recipeInstructions.text: Once the syrup is cool, add the sliced cucumber, shallots, and spur chilies.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 3: Garnish
recipeInstructions.text: Sprinkle with crushed roasted peanuts before serving with hot fish cakes.

31.name: For Stir-Fries: Gai Pad Med Mamuang (Chicken with Cashew Nuts)
31.alt: A plate of stir-fried chicken with cashew nuts and dried chilies.
31.description: A popular stir-fry with a balanced flavor. A little palm sugar enhances the taste of the oyster sauce and chili paste, giving the dish a beautiful glaze.
recipeIngredient: Sliced onion, and spring onions cut into chunks
1 tsp Golden Taan Palm Sugar Powder

recipeInstructions.name: Step 1: Stir-fry Chicken
recipeInstructions.text: Heat oil in a wok, sauté garlic until fragrant. Add the chicken and stir-fry until cooked.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 2: Season
recipeInstructions.text: Add the onion and stir-fry until translucent. Season with oyster sauce, chili paste, and palm sugar. Stir well to combine.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 3: Add Final Ingredients
recipeInstructions.text: Add the cashew nuts, fried dried chilies, and spring onions. Give it a quick toss to combine, then turn off the heat.

32.name: For a Lanna Classic: Khao Soi (Northern Thai Noodle Curry)
32.alt: A rich bowl of Khao Soi with chicken and crispy noodles.
32.description: A Northern Thai noodle dish with a rich flavor from curry paste and coconut milk. Adding a little palm sugar cuts the saltiness and makes the broth's flavor smoother and more well-rounded.
recipeIngredient: Egg noodles (boiled and deep-fried for topping)
1 tbsp Golden Taan Palm Sugar Block

recipeInstructions.name: Step 1: Sauté the Curry Paste
recipeInstructions.text: Sauté a little coconut cream with the Khao Soi curry paste until fragrant and the oil separates.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 2: Simmer the Meat
recipeInstructions.text: Add the chicken or beef and stir-fry with the paste. Then, add the remaining coconut milk. Simmer on low heat until the meat is tender.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 3: Season
recipeInstructions.text: Season with fish sauce and palm sugar. Taste for a balanced flavor. Serve with boiled and crispy noodles, along with side condiments.

33.name: For Grilling: Gai Yang Dipping Sauce (Grilled Chicken Dip)
33.alt: A spicy and savory dipping sauce perfect for Thai grilled chicken.
33.description: An indispensable dipping sauce for grilled chicken and sticky rice. The sweetness of palm sugar, when mixed with tamarind paste and chili flakes, creates a zesty and perfectly balanced flavor.
recipeIngredient: 1.5 tbsp Golden Taan Palm Sugar Powder

recipeInstructions.name: Step 1: Dissolve the Sugar
recipeInstructions.text: In a mixing bowl, add palm sugar, fish sauce, and tamarind paste. Stir until the sugar is well dissolved.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 2: Add Seasonings
recipeInstructions.text: Add the chili flakes and toasted rice powder. Mix well.

recipeInstructions.name: Step 3: Serve
recipeInstructions.text: Garnish with chopped spring onions and coriander. Serve with hot grilled chicken.

1.question: Is your palm sugar 100% natural and authentic?
1.answer: Yes, absolutely. Our sugar is made from 100% pure sap from the Toddy Palm tree (Borassus flabellifer). It is slow-boiled over a traditional wood fire without any additives, mixing agents, or chemical bleaching. What you get is the purest form of palm sugar.

2.question: What is the shelf life of your products?
2.answer:
Our products are packaged to ensure maximum freshness.

*Bars & Powder:* 24 months from the production date.
*Syrup:* 12 months. Please refrigerate after opening to maintain its quality.

3.question: Do you export to the U.S., Europe, or other parts of Asia?
3.answer: Yes. We have extensive experience in global logistics and currently export to multiple regions. We can provide all necessary export documentation (e.g., FDA, Organic, Halal where required). Please contact our export team for details on Minimum Order Quantities (MOQ) and custom packaging.

4.question: Can I request OEM or private label packaging for my brand?
4.answer: Yes. We are fully equipped to support OEM and private label services for our B2B partners. You can choose from our bar, powder, or syrup formats and apply your own branding. Let us be the source of your high-quality, authentic palm sugar product.

5.question: How is Golden Taan different from coconut sugar or regular cane sugar?
5.answer: The difference is in the source, process, and flavor. Golden Taan comes from the Toddy Palm and has a richer, deeper caramel note with a naturally smoky scent and a distinct mineral sweetness. It also has a lower glycemic index. Coconut sugar is milder, and cane sugar is one-dimensionally sweet. Our sugar is favored by chefs and health-conscious consumers for its complex flavor and nutritional benefits.

contact.title: Get In Touch With Golden Taan
contact.subtitle: Have questions about our products, bulk orders, or partnerships? We'd love to hear from you.
contact.addressText: Golden Taan, 96/3 Moo.2, T. Wanyai, A. Bang Phae, Ratchaburi 70160, Thailand.

footer.description: A Legacy of Sweetness from Nature. Bringing the finest, organic palmyra palm sugar from Thailand to the world.

recipesPage.title: Palmyra Palm Sugar Recipes & Usage Ideas
recipesPage.subtitle: From traditional desserts to modern cocktails, discover how to use our natural palm sugar.
recipesPage.noRecipes: There are currently no recipes in this category. Please check back soon!

benefitsPage.title: The Wholesome Difference of a Low GI Sweetener
benefitsPage.subtitle: Why Choose Golden Taan as a Healthy Sugar Alternative?

compareSugars.title: How Does Golden Taan Compare?
compareSugars.subtitle: A side-by-side look at Golden Taan versus other common sweeteners.

table.intro: The key differences lie in the source, nutritional profile, and production process. Here’s a detailed comparison:
table.row1Taan: 100% pure sap from Palmyra Palm (Toddy Palm). Minimally processed; unrefined.
table.row1Common: From sugarcane or sugar beets. Highly refined, bleached, and stripped of molasses.
table.row2Taan: Low (approx. 35). Slower energy release, avoids blood sugar spikes.
table.row2Common: High (approx. 65). Causes rapid blood sugar spikes.
table.row3Taan: Rich in minerals like Potassium, Magnesium, Iron, and Zinc.
table.row3Common: Essentially zero nutritional value beyond empty calories.
table.row4Taan: Complex, rich caramel and butterscotch notes with a subtle smoky finish.
table.row5Taan: None. 100% natural, no anti-caking agents or bleaching chemicals.
table.row5Common: Often processed with chemicals like sulfur dioxide and filtered with bone char.
